projects.title: GITHUB UNIVERSE
projects.shortDescription: An interactive GitHub data visualization universe built with a static-data pipeline. It features a 4-level drill-down (Galaxy -> Country -> Users -> Repositories) driven by deep scraping and query-bucket partitioning.

blocks.content: GitHub Universe is an experimental data visualization project that maps public GitHub activity into an explorable cosmos. Using a custom Python scraper with query-bucket partitioning and deep-scrape follower modes, the pipeline statically compiles millions of data points into a high-performance 4-level SVG and WebGL drill-down interface: Countries (Level 0), Cities (Level 1), Users (Level 2), and Repositories (Level 3).
Visualization UI

items: Static-Data Pipeline: Bypasses runtime backend requirements by compiling scraped data into `data/index.json` and partitioned `data/countries/{CODE}.json` files, achieving zero-latency data serving on Vercel.

Query-Bucket Partitioning: The scraper logic segments API requests by 'follower' and 'year' buckets to circumvent top-result API caps, yielding a higher density of data representation per country node.

Adaptive Rendering: Incorporates an automatic performance degradation engine (auto/high/mid/low) with a live FPS HUD, culling off-screen elements via wide-spread layouts and de-crowding algorithms to maintain 60 FPS in dense views.

blocks.content: The primary objective is to map the vast, invisible landscape of the open-source community into a tangible, explorable cosmos. By treating GitHub not just as a database, but as a digital universe, the visualization allows users to navigate geographically—zooming seamlessly from a macro-galactic view of global countries down into specific cities, and eventually into the individual users (stars) and their repositories (satellites). It transforms raw, tabular API data into an interactive, spatial map of global software engineering density.

The most formidable engineering hurdle was the sheer scale of data retrieval combined with GitHub's strict API rate limits. Scraping a global population sequentially takes an unfeasible amount of time, and the API hard-caps pagination at 1,000 results per query. To bypass this, the scraper was engineered with an advanced "Query-Bucket Partitioning" algorithm. By dynamically slicing search requests into granular sub-buckets (based on exact follower counts and account creation years), the system forces the API to expose deeper layers of hidden users. Additionally, a fault-tolerant state-resume mode was built to handle network drops over continuous multi-day scraping operations.

projects.title: CATSY COFFEE
projects.shortDescription: A multi-platform cafe operations system featuring a FastAPI backend, a React web storefront, and an offline-first Flutter POS. Processes 50+ orders daily and loads in under 1.2s.

blocks.content: A local cafe struggled with dropped orders and slow processing times during frequent internet outages. They relied on manual paper backups that failed to sync with their digital inventory, leading to lost revenue and operational chaos during peak hours.

I decided to build a 3-tier system emphasizing reliability. The core is an offline-first Flutter POS app that seamlessly falls back to a local SQLite database (via Drift) when the connection drops. All transactions are queued locally and synchronized automatically to a Supabase PostgreSQL backend via WebSockets once the internet is restored.

The system successfully eliminated data loss from dropped connections. It reliably processes 50+ orders per day without downtime. The customer-facing React storefront loads in under 1.2s, and leveraging Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM), staff receive instant background notifications for new orders across all devices, streamlining the entire service flow.

projects.title: GANAP PH
projects.shortDescription: An AI-powered livelihood guidance platform designed to bridge unemployed and underemployed residents in Tagum City with personalized career roadmaps and government opportunities.

blocks.content: Many residents in Tagum City are unemployed or underemployed not because they lack skills, but because they lack access to the right guidance. Opportunities like free TESDA training, DOLE assistance, and PESO referrals exist, but government websites are difficult to navigate for ordinary users with low digital literacy.

GanapPH transforms confusing government information into a clear, step-by-step action plan. By asking simple onboarding questions (skills, location, work preference, and target income), the AI generates a personalized livelihood roadmap recommending training programs, local job opportunities, and microbusiness paths.

Anthony, 62, a vendor and former security guard with limited digital literacy. He struggles finding stable income and finds government processes confusing. He needs clear direction to improve his technical skills.
Wilmar, 21, a college student balancing studies with work. He struggles finding flexible job opportunities and wants a clearer direction toward stable employment after graduation.
Noel and Cherly, business managers relying on referrals and Facebook postings. They struggle to find workers that match their required skills and want to simplify local hiring.

items: TAM: 2.26 million unemployed Filipinos nationwide who could benefit from opportunity access.
SAM: 5.3 million residents across the Davao Region.
SOM: 300,000+ residents in Tagum City, with an initial pilot reach of 500-1000 users.

projects.title: BAYLORA
projects.shortDescription: A modern mobile marketplace and trading platform built with Flutter and Supabase. Facilitates secure cash, trade, or mixed transactions with a real-time bidding system.

blocks.content: Baylora is a flexible marketplace application that revives the bartering system for the digital age. It allows users to list items for straight cash, trade, or a mixed combination, supported by a real-time bidding and offer-management system.

The application heavily utilizes Supabase as a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) to manage relational PostgreSQL data, securely authenticate users, and store media uploads efficiently, allowing the frontend logic to be cleanly managed by Riverpod.

proofPoints.label: Core Flow
proofPoints.value: Cash, trade, and mixed bidding transactions

projects.title: NLP CLASSIFIER
projects.shortDescription: A specialized machine learning pipeline for topic classification and language detection across English, Tagalog, and Cebuano using BERTopic and BART models.

blocks.content: This project tackles the complexity of multi-dialect natural language processing. It combines a custom language detection model with advanced Transformer-based topic extractors to accurately classify unstructured text in code-switched environments.

The backend API leverages FastAPI to serve heavily cached .pkl model states. By pre-training the Logistic Regression, LDA, and BERTopic embeddings, the system reduces cold-start latency and can route input text to the correct model based on instant language identification.
The custom supervised Language Detection model achieved highly reliable accuracy across English, Tagalog, and Cebuano texts, forming a solid baseline before text is routed to topic modeling.

blocks.caption: Comparative accuracy of supervised models in the pipeline.

projects.title: KERNEL
projects.shortDescription: A high-end scroll-triggered digital experience built on React and GSAP. Employs a 'Prism & Glass' design system featuring a 40px blueprint grid and glowing cyan (#00ffcc) accents over a void navy (#0a0f1a) background.

blocks.content: KERNEL is a sophisticated React-based framework designed for immersive, scroll-driven storytelling. It blends a 'Terminal-Glass' aesthetic with elite performance, serving as the core engine for high-end digital experiences.
Motion Engine

items: GSAP Viewport Pining — A critical viewport override was implemented—enforcing overflow-x: hidden strictly on the <html> tag—to prevent positioning recalculation errors and broken layouts during complex fixed-pinning phases.
Lenis Integration — Synchronized native GSAP scroll triggers with Lenis to ensure ultra-smooth, deterministic hardware-accelerated interpolation.
Audio-Active Sensory Loop — Implemented real-time synchronization between the user's scroll velocity and ambient acoustic humming for deep immersion.

fonts.name: Playfair Display
fonts.role: High-fashion editorial narrative and display headers

fonts.name: JetBrains Mono
fonts.role: System logs, data UI elements, and technical diagnostics

items: Blueprint Geometry — Relies on a strict 40px background grid generated purely via CSS linear-gradients to maintain the technical schematic aesthetic.

projects.title: VAYORA
projects.shortDescription: A bespoke luxury travel curation platform built with React 18 and Framer Motion, featuring immersive micro-interactions and an elegant design system.

blocks.content: Vayora is an exclusive travel curation application focusing on luxury itineraries and high-end concierge services. Drawing inspiration from platforms like Airbnb Luxe and Black Tomato, the architecture leverages a modern React/Vite stack with highly optimized UI primitives (Radix UI) to deliver a seamless, premium web experience without compromising on performance.
Interaction Libraries

items: Component Primitives — Integrated Radix UI via shadcn/ui to ensure fully accessible, unstyled core components that were subsequently themed with the bespoke Vayora design system.

Fluid Animations — Implemented Framer Motion and tailwindcss-animate to orchestrate smooth entrance reveals ('Fade In Up'), continuous 'Ticker' scroll galleries, and hardware-accelerated drawer transitions.
Strict Typings — Enforced full strict-mode TypeScript across all React components and data interfaces to guarantee runtime safety during itinerary rendering.

fonts.name: Jost
fonts.role: Clean and modern UI elements and navigation

proofPoints.label: UI
proofPoints.value: Responsive luxury layout and motion system

projects.title: BANANA LEAF DETECTOR
projects.shortDescription: A premium Flask web application featuring active learning and computer vision to detect banana leaf diseases. Achieves 95% model accuracy with instant local model retraining.

blocks.content: Traditional ML crop diagnostic tools rely on heavy Deep Learning models that are expensive to run and slow on low-bandwidth rural connections. Additionally, farmers had no way to correct the model when it was wrong, meaning false positives would persist indefinitely.

To bypass the need for a CNN, I built a custom pipeline using OpenCV and Scikit-Image to extract 59 unique color, texture, and structural features from leaf photos. These dense vectors are passed to a lightweight K-Nearest Neighbors (k=5) classifier. To fix the feedback issue, I engineered a closed-loop retraining thread where user corrections instantly append to Firestore and retrain the local KNN model in under 200ms.

The engineered 59-dimensional feature matrix allowed the KNN model to achieve 95% model accuracy in testing. By avoiding heavy deep learning dependencies, the active learning pipeline runs flawlessly on lightweight edge servers. Corrected predictions converge into the live model in less than 200ms, creating a constantly improving diagnostic tool without manual developer intervention.
The K-Nearest Neighbors classifier demonstrates strong generalization, particularly in distinguishing healthy leaves from diseased ones without the overhead of deep learning architectures.

blocks.caption: Confusion Matrix showing strong diagonal performance across all 3 classes.

blocks.content: Integrates a closed-loop active learning system where classifier errors are immediately corrected by the user. The corrected sample's 59-dimensional feature matrix is dynamically appended to the training set and triggers background scikit-learn KNN (k=5) model retraining in under 200ms with live model weights updates in-process, allowing continuous classifier improvements.

Extracts 59 unique color, texture, and structural features from uploaded crop images on-the-fly. Leverages Grey-Level Co-occurrence Matrices (GLCM) with 64 levels at a pixel distance of 5 for texture, Local Binary Patterns (LBP) with P=8 and R=1 for disease-spot boundaries, and HSV/LAB color Moments alongside a 16-bin Hue histogram for rapid chlorosis screening.

items: Predictive Latency: < 80ms for complete OpenCV feature extraction and KNN classification on local hosts.
In-Process Retraining Convergence: < 200ms background scikit-learn KNN fit cycle following user correction events.
Edge Friendly Footprint: Bypasses heavy deep learning dependencies, allowing the entire application and active learning pipeline to run smoothly on extremely lightweight compute servers.
Analytical Load Times: Highly-optimized NoSQL database schema enables instant aggregation of thousands of historical scan entries for Chart.js canvas elements.

projects.title: PIXVAULT
projects.shortDescription: A premium design resource SPA aggregating GIFs, icons, UI components, and illustrations. Built with React 18 and a cyberpunk design system.

blocks.content: PIXVAULT is a single-page application that centralizes design resources for developers and designers. It pulls live data from multiple external APIs — GIFs from Reddit, icons from Iconify, UI components from shadcn/ui, and illustrations from open sources — and presents them in a unified, searchable interface. No accounts required, no paywalls.
State & Data
UI & Animation

items: TanStack Query handles all API fetching, caching, and background refetching — no manual loading state management
Zod schemas validate all external API responses at runtime to prevent runtime crashes from shape changes
shadcn/ui primitives used for accessible components — no custom modal/dropdown logic needed
React Router with lazy-loaded routes keeps initial bundle small
Framer Motion used only for entrance animations — no layout animations that could cause reflow

blocks.content: GIFs are pulled live from Reddit's top GIF communities with search and category filtering. Icons are sourced from Iconify — covering Lucide, Material, Heroicons, Tabler, and Phosphor — with copy-SVG and download functionality.

Pre-built shadcn/ui components are displayed with copy-paste code snippets. Each component is fully typed, WCAG-accessible, and Tailwind-customizable. The inspiration feed aggregates curated design content from multiple sources.

PIXVAULT ships with a complete design system prompt for AI agents. Copy it into any AI conversation to instantly replicate the dark cyberpunk aesthetic — colors, typography, component patterns, spacing, and visual effects — in any new project.

items: TanStack Query caches all API responses — repeat visits load instantly with stale-while-revalidate
Lazy-loaded routes — only the active section's JS is loaded on navigation
All images lazy-loaded with placeholder shimmer animations
Framer Motion animations use transform/opacity only — no layout triggers
Deployed on Vercel with edge CDN — sub-100ms TTFB globally
